project_outputs.title: Clinical decision support use case specification
Manufacturing use case and KPI specification
Logistics demonstrator specification and benchmark report

outcomes.title: Logistics and supply chain transfer demonstrator (TRL 5)

impact_pathways.impact_narrative: MAESTRO implements the European Apply AI Strategy across its three prioritised economic sectors through three sector-dedicated, TRL-5 validated demonstrators. The NIHS-led clinical decision support demonstrator (WP5) deploys multi-specialist-agent AI coordination on 500 de-identified clinical cases with HL7 FHIR integration, producing a validated system with auditable reasoning traces supporting EU AI Act high-risk system compliance requirements, and targeting engagement of ≥3 hospital networks in formal post-project adoption planning by M48 (D5-01, D5-02). The ISIA-led manufacturing demonstrator (WP6) deploys multi-agent process optimisation with digital twin integration in an operational factory environment across T6-01 through T6-03, targeting ≥15% defect reduction and ≥10% energy savings against pre-defined KPIs (D6-01, D6-02). The ELI-led logistics transfer demonstrator (WP7) validates MAESTRO architecture generalisation across three international supply chain corridors and produces a formal architectural transferability mapping to in-vehicle autonomous driving, confirming MAESTRO's demonstrated reach across all three Apply AI Strategy sectors and evidencing the portability required by EI-01 (D7-01, D7-02).

MAESTRO operationalises the EDIH-mediated industrial deployment pathway through three complementary channels. First, the validated healthcare demonstrator dataset (D5-02, OUT-4) is disseminated to European hospital networks through the consortium EDIH network, with EDIH-mediated adoption planning targeting ≥3 hospital networks by M48; the EDIH dissemination route is explicitly documented in IMP-2 as the primary deployment mechanism to hospital networks across Europe. Second, the manufacturing demonstrator software (D6-02, OUT-5) is transferred to ≥5 manufacturing SMEs via ≥2 EDIHs, with EIT Manufacturing network engagement amplifying reach beyond direct consortium contacts (IMP-3). Third, the open-source MAESTRO framework (D9-02, OUT-8) and benchmark suite (D8-02, OUT-7) are published on the AI-on-demand platform, enabling ≥10 European SMEs and startups to build next-generation AI agent applications by M48 without dependency on non-European platforms (IMP-5). The BAL-led WP9 exploitation strategy coordinates EDIH partnership agreements and monitors industrial uptake metrics across all three channels.

outcomes.title: Neuro-symbolic planning framework for LLM-based agents
Formally verified multi-agent coordination protocol suite
External Tool and API Orchestration Layer (TRL 5)

impact_pathways.impact_narrative: MAESTRO delivers open scientific advancement through two reinforcing channels targeting the European research community and the AI-on-demand platform. The first channel is the MAESTRO publication stream: ≥20 peer-reviewed publications in top AI venues (NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR, AAMAS, AAAI) disseminate the neuro-symbolic planning engine (D2-02, WP2-ATU), adaptive memory system (D3-02, WP3-BIIS), and formally verified multi-agent coordination protocols (D4-02, WP4-CERIA), targeting ≥100 citations within three years of publication and ≥3 invited keynotes at major AI conferences (IMP-1). The second channel is platform contribution: the evaluation framework and benchmark specification (D8-01, WP8-FIIT), benchmark suite dataset (D8-02), and open-source framework release (D9-02, WP9-BAL) are contributed to the AI-on-demand platform as freely reusable research infrastructure with reproducible baselines, targeting adoption by ≥5 external research groups. Together, these outputs strengthen shared European AI research infrastructure for next-generation agent development and disseminate advances that are foundational for the European AI research community.

Formally verified multi-agent coordination protocol suite
External Tool and API Orchestration Layer (TRL 5)

impact_pathways.impact_narrative: MAESTRO strengthens EU open strategic autonomy in digital and emerging enabling technologies by establishing European-origin foundational capabilities for next-generation AI agents that provide sovereign alternatives to scale-only approaches from non-European platforms. The neuro-symbolic planning engine (D2-02, WP2-ATU), adaptive memory system (D3-02, WP3-BIIS), and formally verified coordination protocols (D4-01, D4-02, WP4-CERIA) constitute a coherent, open, European-origin AI agent architecture — differentiated from US and Chinese approaches by its formal verifiability and auditability rather than parameter scale alone (IMP-1). The open-source MAESTRO framework release (D9-02) and evaluation infrastructure (D8-01) ensure these capabilities are accessible to European researchers and companies without platform lock-in. CERIA's formally specified coordination protocol deliverable (D4-01) serves as the basis for a ≥1 standardisation submission to ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 42 or IEEE (IMP-6), accompanied by an EU AI Act multi-agent transparency alignment report — directly extending EU strategic autonomy from research output into international AI governance norm-setting. Note: This pathway addresses an Inferred expected impact derived from topic destination placement; no explicit impact statement for strategic autonomy appeared in the call scope or expected_outcome sections.

tier2b_source_ref.inference_note: EI-04 is inferred from destination placement only. Pathway substantively grounded in impacts.json:IMP-1 and IMP-6. No explicit expected-impact statement for strategic autonomy confirmed in call_entry.scope or call_entry.expected_outcome.

outcomes.title: Neuro-symbolic planning framework for LLM-based agents
Formally verified multi-agent coordination protocol suite
External Tool and API Orchestration Layer (TRL 5)

impact_pathways.impact_narrative: MAESTRO unlocks the economic potential of AI agents across diverse sectors — including data analytics, coding, and domain-specific autonomous task execution — by removing the three foundational architectural bottlenecks that constrain current deployment. The neuro-symbolic planning engine (D2-02, WP2-ATU) enables AI agents to decompose complex multi-step goals into verifiable sub-task graphs with formal pre/post-conditions, making agents economically deployable in analytical and coding workflows where execution failures are costly. The adaptive tri-store memory system (D3-02, WP3-BIIS) enables long-horizon agent coherence across extended interaction sequences without retraining, unlocking deployment in financial analysis, legal research, and software development. The external tool and API orchestration layer (D8-01, OUT-9, WP2/WP8) provides typed tool registry, formal tool selection policies, and post-invocation verification enabling reliable AI agent integration with external APIs and services — a prerequisite for economically deployable agentic workflows across any sector. The MAESTRO benchmark suite (D8-02, OUT-7) and open-source framework (D9-02, OUT-8) make these advances accessible to ≥10 European SMEs/startups by M48, targeting ≥500 GitHub stars within 6 months of the M36 release, directly realising the call's expected economic potential across diverse sectors including data analytics and coding.

kpis.description: Hospital networks engaged in post-project clinical AI adoption planning
kpis.target: ≥3 hospital networks with formal adoption planning documented by M48
kpis.measurement_method: Stakeholder engagement audit and signed letters of intent from hospital network representatives, verified against clinical validation outcomes in D5-02 and EDIH dissemination reports from WP9

kpis.description: Defect reduction and energy savings demonstrated in pilot factory operational validation
kpis.target: ≥15% defect reduction AND ≥10% energy savings relative to pre-deployment baseline in ISIA pilot factory
kpis.measurement_method: Factory operational KPI report from WP6 T6-03 comparing pre- and post-MAESTRO-deployment production metrics across defined production segment KPI framework

kpis.description: Supply chain disruption recovery time improvement operationally demonstrated across international logistics corridors
kpis.target: ≥20% improvement in disruption recovery time across ≥3 international corridors in ELI operational environment
kpis.measurement_method: Operational logistics data analysis in WP7 T7-03 comparing agent-assisted versus baseline disruption recovery scenarios, captured in D7-02 evaluation dataset

kpis.description: European SMEs and startups building production applications on the MAESTRO open-source framework
kpis.target: ≥10 European SMEs/startups with active MAESTRO-based development by M48
kpis.measurement_method: GitHub repository dependency tracking for MAESTRO framework packages, supplemented by EDIH engagement reports produced under WP9 T9-01 confirming active SME adoption

kpis.description: EDIHs actively disseminating MAESTRO technology to industrial beneficiaries
kpis.target: ≥3 EDIHs with confirmed active MAESTRO dissemination activities by M48
kpis.measurement_method: Signed EDIH partnership agreements and WP9-produced activity reports evidencing active technology transfer to industrial beneficiaries, cross-referenced with D8-02 platform contribution records

kpis.description: Peer-reviewed publications in top-tier AI research venues
kpis.target: ≥20 publications in NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR, AAMAS, or AAAI by M60
kpis.measurement_method: Publication tracking via Scopus and DBLP with venue tier classification; publications logged in dissemination register maintained under WP9 T9-03 and reported against D8-01 evaluation framework

kpis.description: External research groups formally adopting MAESTRO benchmark suite as evaluation infrastructure
kpis.target: ≥5 external research groups actively using MAESTRO benchmark suite by M48
kpis.measurement_method: GitHub repository fork and adoption metrics, benchmark leaderboard registrations, and formal citation tracking identifying D8-02 as evaluation infrastructure in external publications

kpis.description: Formal standardisation proposal submitted to international multi-agent AI standardisation body
kpis.target: ≥1 formal submission to ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 42 or IEEE Standards Association by M48
kpis.measurement_method: Formal submission confirmation and tracking number issued by standardisation body, based on D4-01 protocol specification and reported by CERIA in WP4 activities

kpis.description: Citations of MAESTRO publications within three years of publication date
kpis.target: ≥100 citations aggregated across MAESTRO publication portfolio, measured 12 months post-project end
kpis.measurement_method: Bibliometric analysis via Scopus and Google Scholar with publication date-normalised citation counts, reported in final dissemination impact report linked to D9-02

kpis.description: GitHub stars on MAESTRO open-source framework within six months of release
kpis.target: ≥500 GitHub stars within 6 months of M36 open-source release
kpis.measurement_method: GitHub repository statistics tracked monthly from release date under WP9 T9-01 open-source release management activities, reported against D9-02

kpis.description: MAESTRO agent architecture performance versus state-of-the-art baselines on standardised benchmark suite across planning, memory, and coordination task categories
kpis.target: Statistically significant improvement (p<0.05) over SOTA baselines on ≥3 task category dimensions in MAESTRO benchmark suite
kpis.measurement_method: Cross-demonstrator benchmark evaluation results produced in WP8 T8-03, reported in final integration and evaluation report (D8-03), with task category results covering planning decomposition, memory coherence, and multi-agent coordination
kpis.tier3_source: outcomes.json:OUT-7.description (benchmark suite with standardised metrics and reproducible evaluation protocols)

dissemination_plan.open_access_policy: All MAESTRO peer-reviewed scientific publications will be deposited in open-access repositories (Zenodo and institutional repositories of the contributing partner) under Plan S-compliant CC-BY licence within six months of acceptance, in full compliance with Horizon Europe open science mandates. The MAESTRO software framework, benchmark suite, coordination protocol specifications, and evaluation datasets will be released under the Apache 2.0 open-source licence on GitHub and contributed to the AI-on-demand platform, ensuring FAIR (Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, Reusable) accessibility for the European research and innovation community. A data management plan deliverable will be produced within the first six months of the grant, governing data classification, FAIR compliance, and open access timelines for all project outputs. Sensitive clinical validation data from WP5 will be anonymised in accordance with GDPR and EU AI Act requirements before any public release, with the de-identified evaluation dataset (D5-02) published as open data.

activities.expected_result: Formal post-project AI agent deployment adoption planning agreements with ≥3 European hospital networks, documenting a regulatory-compliant pathway for clinical decision support integration grounded in the D5-02 validated evaluation dataset and the EU AI Act high-risk system compliance evidence produced in WP5

Operational technology transfer of the manufacturing process optimisation demonstrator (D6-02, OUT-5) to ≥5 European manufacturing SMEs via ≥2 EDIH partnerships and the EIT Manufacturing network, establishing consulting and licensing revenue streams for ISIA in AI-driven factory optimisation and demonstrating ≥15% defect reduction and ≥10% energy savings as transferable commercial propositions

A confirmed integration roadmap for MAESTRO multi-agent coordination capabilities into ELI's operational supply chain management systems, targeting commercial deployment across ≥3 international logistics corridors and establishing ELI as an anchor European commercial operator for AI agent-driven supply chain resilience

≥10 European SMEs and startups actively building production AI agent applications on the MAESTRO open-source framework by M48, with ≥500 GitHub stars within 6 months of the M36 release, creating a self-sustaining European AI agent application ecosystem that reduces platform dependency on non-European proprietary solutions and generates downstream commercial AI agent products

MAESTRO formally verified coordination protocol specification (D4-01) adopted by ≥3 external multi-agent AI research projects as a reference architecture, with ≥1 formal standardisation submission to ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 42 or IEEE Standards Association received and tracked, extending MAESTRO's commercial and regulatory reach through normative embedding

exploitation_plan.ipr_strategy: The MAESTRO consortium adopts an open-core IPR strategy calibrated to maximise European ecosystem adoption while preserving commercial exploitation pathways for sector-specific industrial partners. The core MAESTRO framework, benchmark suite, evaluation infrastructure, and coordination protocol specifications are released under the Apache 2.0 open-source licence, jointly owned by the consortium, to lower barriers for European SMEs and startups and to support the AI-on-demand platform contribution mandate. Sector-specific demonstrator software configurations, clinical validation datasets (subject to GDPR anonymisation), and industrial deployment parameters may be protected as trade secrets or licensed under commercial terms by the respective responsible partners: NIHS for the clinical decision support configuration, ISIA for the manufacturing optimisation deployment, and ELI for the logistics integration. CERIA retains attribution for the formally verified coordination protocol specification submitted to standardisation bodies, with consortium consent. BAL leads IPR management coordination under WP9 and maintains the consortium IP register, ensuring that open-source release decisions are governed by the consortium agreement and do not unintentionally foreclose commercial licensing paths for any partner.

sustainability_mechanism.description: MAESTRO's post-project sustainability is grounded in three reinforcing mechanisms, each anchored in documented project outputs and specific consortium partner commitments. First, the open-source MAESTRO framework released on GitHub and the AI-on-demand platform (D9-02, OUT-8, BAL) creates a self-sustaining community maintenance and extension dynamic: once ≥10 European SMEs and startups build production applications on the framework by M48, their operational dependency generates continuous community-driven development, issue reporting, and contribution activity that maintains and extends the technology independent of project funding. Second, operational embedding in commercial environments — NIHS deploying clinical decision support in hospital networks, ISIA integrating manufacturing optimisation in operational factory systems, and ELI running multi-agent logistics coordination across international supply corridors — creates post-project maintenance obligations driven by live operational requirements, ensuring that the technology is sustained through each partner's existing commercial and operational revenue streams rather than grant continuations. Third, CERIA's standardisation submissions to ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 42 or IEEE establish MAESTRO coordination protocol specifications as a normative international reference, ensuring the coordination architecture survives in the standards landscape independently of any single organisation's continued involvement. BAL coordinates the long-term exploitation strategy through WP9, maintains EDIH partnership agreements into post-project operations, and produces the final exploitation and dissemination impact report (D9-02) documenting the handover of community governance to the open-source project maintainer structure.
